headline: Industrial Pollution Linked to Cancer Clusters in 4 Districts
context: Epidemiological studies identified statistically significant cancer clusters near industrial zones in 4 Malaysian districts.

cards.big: Cancer rates in 4 industrial districts are 2.3 to 3.1 times the national average
cards.sub: Pasir Gudang Gebeng Kulim and Bintulu all show elevated cancer incidence

cards.big: DOE air and water monitoring at industrial zones covers only 45% of operational factories
cards.sub: Monitoring gaps mean emission levels at most factories are unknown

cards.big: Affected communities filed 340 health complaints since 2020 with no epidemiological investigation
cards.sub: Absence of health impact assessments means causal links remain officially unconfirmed

cards.big: Erin Brockovich case in US led to mandatory health impact assessments near industrial zones
cards.sub: Community health surveillance around industrial areas prevents long-term population harm

cards.big: Communities should not have to prove they are being poisoned—industries should prove they are not
Should mandatory health impact assessments be required for all communities near industrial zones
